query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx-datetime repository.
The codebase is at version 0.6 (commit: 11c51576).

## Issue to Resolve

The parsing API accepts non-ASCII digits
```kotlin
LocalDate.parse("2ï¼گ2ï¼”ظ ظ©ï¼گà¥¬", LocalDate.Formats.ISO_BASIC) == LocalDate(2024, 9, 6)
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `core/common/src/internal/format/FormatStructure.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/parser/NumberConsumer.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/format/parser/ParserOperation.kt`
- `core/common/src/internal/util.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

1â†’/*
  2â†’ * Copyright 2023 JetBrains s.r.o. and contributors.
  3â†’ * Use of this source code is governed by the Apache 2.0 License that can be found in the LICENSE.txt file.
  4â†’ */
  5â†’
  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser
  7â†’
  8â†’internal interface ParserOperation<in Output> {
  9â†’    fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult
 10â†’}
 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Consumes exactly the string [string].
 14â†’ */
 15â†’internal class PlainStringParserOperation<Output>(val string: String) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 16â†’    init {
 17â†’        require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Empty string is not allowed" }
 18â†’        require(!string[0].isDigit()) { "String '$string' starts with a digit" }
 19â†’        require(!string[string.length - 1].isDigit()) { "String '$string' ends with a digit" }
 20â†’    }
 21â†’
 22â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 23â†’        if (startIndex + string.length > input.length)
 24â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse '$string'" }
 25â†’        for (i in string.indices) {
 26â†’            if (input[startIndex + i] != string[i]) return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 27â†’                "Expected $string but got ${input.substring(startIndex, startIndex + i + 1)}"
 28â†’            }
 29â†’        }
 30â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + string.length)
 31â†’    }
 32â†’
 33â†’    // TODO: properly escape
 34â†’    override fun toString(): String = "'$string'"
 35â†’}
 36â†’
 37â†’/**
 38â†’ * Greedily consumes as many digits as possible and redistributes them among [consumers].
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * At most one consumer is allowed to have a variable length.
 41â†’ * If more digits are supplied than the sum of lengths of all consumers, this is the consumer that will receive the
 42â†’ * extra.
 43â†’ */
 44â†’internal class NumberSpanParserOperation<Output>(
 45â†’    val consumers: List<NumberConsumer<Output>>,
 46â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 47â†’
 48â†’    private val minLength = consumers.sumOf { it.length ?: 1 }
 49â†’    private val isFlexible = consumers.any { it.length == null }
 50â†’
 51â†’    init {
 52â†’        require(consumers.all { (it.length ?: Int.MAX_VALUE) > 0 })
 53â†’        require(consumers.count { it.length == null } <= 1) {
 54â†’            val fieldNames = consumers.filter { it.length == null }.map { it.whatThisExpects }
 55â†’            "At most one variable-length numeric field in a row is allowed, but got several: $fieldNames. " +
 56â†’                "Parsing is undefined: for example, with variable-length month number " +
 57â†’                "and variable-length day of month, '111' can be parsed as Jan 11th or Nov 1st."
 58â†’        }
 59â†’    }
 60â†’
 61â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String
 62â†’        get() {
 63â†’            val consumerLengths = consumers.map {
 64â†’                when (val length = it.length) {
 65â†’                    null -> "at least one digit"
 66â†’                    else -> "$length digits"
 67â†’                } + " for ${it.whatThisExpects}"
 68â†’            }
 69â†’            return if (isFlexible) {
 70â†’                "a number with at least $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 71â†’            } else {
 72â†’                "a number with exactly $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 73â†’            }
 74â†’        }
 75â†’
 76â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 77â†’        if (startIndex + minLength > input.length)
 78â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse $whatThisExpects" }
 79â†’        var digitsInRow = 0
 80â†’        while (startIndex + digitsInRow < input.length && input[startIndex + digitsInRow].isDigit()) {
 81â†’            ++digitsInRow
 82â†’        }
 83â†’        if (digitsInRow < minLength)
 84â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 85â†’                "Only found $digitsInRow digits in a row, but need to parse $whatThisExpects"
 86â†’            }
 87â†’        var index = startIndex
 88â†’        for (i in consumers.indices) {
 89â†’            val length = consumers[i].length ?: (digitsInRow - minLength + 1)
 90â†’            val numberString = input.substring(index, index + length)
 91â†’            val error = consumers[i].consume(storage, numberString)
 92â†’            if (error != null) {
 93â†’                return ParseResult.Error(index) {
 94â†’                    "Can not interpret the string '$numberString' as ${consumers[i].whatThisExpects}: ${error.errorMessage()}"
 95â†’                }
 96â†’            }
 97â†’            index += length
 98â†’        }
 99â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(index)
100â†’    }
101â†’
102â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
103â†’}
104â†’
105â†’internal class SignParser<Output>(
106â†’    private val isNegativeSetter: (Output, Boolean) -> Unit,
107â†’    private val withPlusSign: Boolean,
108â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
109â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
110â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
111â†’        if (startIndex >= input.length)
112â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
113â†’        val char = input[startIndex]
114â†’        if (char == '-') {
115â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, true)
116â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
117â†’        }
118â†’        if (char == '+' && withPlusSign) {
119â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, false)
120â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
121â†’        }
122â†’        return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got $char" }
123â†’    }
124â†’
125â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
126â†’}
127â†’
128â†’/**
129â†’ * Consumes an empty string and unconditionally modifies the accumulator.
130â†’ */
131â†’internal class UnconditionalModification<Output>(
132â†’    private val operation: (Output) -> Unit
133â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
134â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
135â†’        operation(storage)
136â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
137â†’    }
138â†’}
139â†’
140â†’/**
141â†’ * Matches the longest suitable string from `strings` and calls [consume] with the matched string.
142â†’ */
143â†’internal class StringSetParserOperation<Output>(
144â†’    strings: Collection<String>,
145â†’    private val setter: AssignableField<Output, String>,
146â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
147â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
148â†’
149â†’    // TODO: tries don't have good performance characteristics for small sets, add a special case for small sets
150â†’
151â†’    private class TrieNode(
152â†’        val children: MutableList<Pair<String, TrieNode>> = mutableListOf(),
153â†’        var isTerminal: Boolean = false
154â†’    )
155â†’
156â†’    private val trie = TrieNode()
157â†’
158â†’    init {
159â†’        for (string in strings) {
160â†’            require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Found an empty string in $whatThisExpects" }
161â†’            var node = trie
162â†’            for (char in string) {
163â†’                val searchResult = node.children.binarySearchBy(char.toString()) { it.first }
164â†’                node = if (searchResult < 0) {
165â†’                    TrieNode().also { node.children.add(-searchResult - 1, char.toString() to it) }
166â†’                } else {
167â†’                    node.children[searchResult].second
168â†’                }
169â†’            }
170â†’            require(!node.isTerminal) { "The string '$string' was passed several times" }
171â†’            node.isTerminal = true
172â†’        }
173â†’        fun reduceTrie(trie: TrieNode) {
174â†’            for ((_, child) in trie.children) {
175â†’                reduceTrie(child)
176â†’            }
177â†’            val newChildren = mutableListOf<Pair<String, TrieNode>>()
178â†’            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
179â†’                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
180â†’                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
181â†’                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
182â†’                } else {
183â†’                    newChildren.add(key to child)
184â†’                }
185â†’            }
186â†’            trie.children.clear()
187â†’            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
188â†’        }
189â†’        reduceTrie(trie)
190â†’    }
191â†’
192â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
193â†’        var node = trie
194â†’        var index = startIndex
195â†’        var lastMatch: Int? = null
196â†’        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
197â†’            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
198â†’            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
199â†’                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
200â†’                    node = child
201â†’                    index += key.length
202â†’                    continue@loop
203â†’                }
204â†’            }
205â†’            break // nothing found
206â†’        }
207â†’        return if (lastMatch != null) {
208â†’            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
209â†’        } else {
210â†’            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
211â†’        }
212â†’    }
213â†’}
214â†’
215â†’internal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
216â†’    minDigits: Int?,
217â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
218â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
219â†’    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
220â†’    name: String,
221â†’    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
222â†’): ParserStructure<Output> {
223â†’    val parsers = mutableListOf(
224â†’        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
225â†’    )
226â†’    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
227â†’        parsers.add(
228â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
229â†’        )
230â†’        parsers.add(
231â†’            ParserStructure(
232â†’                listOf(
233â†’                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
234â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
235â†’                        listOf(
236â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
237â†’                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
238â†’                                maxDigits,
239â†’                                setter,
240â†’                                name,
241â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
242â†’                            )
243â†’                        )
244â†’                    )
245â†’                ),
246â†’                emptyList()
247â†’            )
248â†’        )
249â†’    } else {
250â†’        parsers.add(
251â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
252â†’        )
253â†’    }
254â†’    return ParserStructure(
255â†’        emptyList(),
256â†’        parsers,
257â†’    )
258â†’}
259â†’
260â†’// With maxWidth = 4,
261â†’// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
262â†’// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
263â†’internal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
264â†’    minDigits: Int?,
265â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
266â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
267â†’    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
268â†’    name: String,
269â†’    withMinus: Boolean = false,
270â†’): ParserStructure<Target> {
271â†’    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
272â†’    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
273â†’    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
274â†’    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
275â†’        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
276â†’        return ParserStructure(
277â†’            buildList {
278â†’                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
279â†’                add(
280â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
281â†’                        listOf(
282â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
283â†’                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
284â†’                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
285â†’                                setter,
286â†’                                name,
287â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
288â†’                            )
289â†’                        )
290â†’                    )
291â†’                )
292â†’            },
293â†’            emptyList()
294â†’        )
295â†’    }
296â†’
297â†’    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)
298â†’    if (minNumberLength >= maxPaddedNumberLength) return numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, maxNumberLength)
299â†’    // invariant: the length of the string parsed by 'accumulated' is exactly 'accumulatedWidth'
300â†’    var accumulated: ParserStructure<Target> = numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, minNumberLength)
301â†’    for (accumulatedWidth in minNumberLength until maxPaddedNumberLength) {
302â†’        accumulated = ParserStructure(
303â†’            emptyList(),
304â†’            listOf(
305â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(accumulatedWidth + 1, accumulatedWidth + 1),
306â†’                listOf(
307â†’                    ParserStructure(listOf(PlainStringParserOperation(" ")), emptyList()),
308â†’                    accumulated
309â†’                ).concat()
310â†’            )
311â†’        )
312â†’    }
313â†’    // accumulatedWidth == maxNumberLength || accumulatedWidth == spacePadding.
314â†’    // In the first case, we're done, in the second case, we need to add the remaining numeric lengths.
315â†’    return if (spacePadding > maxNumberLength) {
316â†’        val prepadding = PlainStringParserOperation<Target>(" ".repeat(spacePadding - maxNumberLength))
317â†’        listOf(
318â†’            ParserStructure(
319â†’                listOf(
320â†’                    prepadding,
321â†’                ),
322â†’                emptyList()
323â†’            ), accumulated
324â†’        ).concat()
325â†’    } else if (spacePadding == maxNumberLength) {
326â†’        accumulated
327â†’    } else {
328â†’        val r = ParserStructure(
329â†’            emptyList(),
330â†’            listOf(
331â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(spacePadding + 1, maxNumberLength),
332â†’                accumulated
333â†’            )
334â†’        )
335â†’        r
336â†’    }
337â†’}
338â†’
339â†’internal fun <Output> ReducedIntParser(
340â†’    digits: Int,
341â†’    base: Int,
342â†’    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
343â†’    name: String,
344â†’): ParserStructure<Output> = ParserStructure(
345â†’    emptyList(),
346â†’    listOf(
347â†’        ParserStructure(
348â†’            listOf(
349â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
350â†’                    listOf(
351â†’                        ReducedIntConsumer(
352â†’                            digits,
353â†’                            setter,
354â†’                            name,
355â†’                            base = base
356â†’                        )
357â†’                    )
358â†’                )
359â†’            ),
360â†’            emptyList()
361â†’        ),
362â†’        ParserStructure(
363â†’            listOf(
364â†’                PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
365â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
366â†’                    listOf(
367â†’                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
368â†’                            null,
369â†’                            null,
370â†’                            setter,
371â†’                            name,
372â†’                            multiplyByMinus1 = false
373â†’                        )
374â†’                    )
375â†’                )
376â†’            ),
377â†’            emptyList()
378â†’        ),
379â†’        ParserStructure(
380â†’            listOf(
381â†’                PlainStringParserOperation("-"),
382â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
383â†’                    listOf(
384â†’                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
385â†’                            null,
386â†’                            null,
387â†’                            setter,
388â†’                            name,
389â†’                            multiplyByMinus1 = true
390â†’                        )
391â†’                    )
392â†’                )
393â†’            ),
394â†’            emptyList()
395â†’        ),
396â†’    )
397â†’)
398â†’
399â†’internal interface AssignableField<in Object, Type> {
400â†’    /**
401â†’     * If the field is not set, sets it to the given value.
402â†’     * If the field is set to the given value, does nothing.
403â†’     * If the field is set to a different value, throws [IllegalArgumentException].
404â†’     *
405â†’     * This function is used to ensure internal consistency during parsing.
406â†’     * There exist formats where the same data is repeated several times in the same object, for example,
407â†’     * "14:15 (02:15 PM)". In such cases, we want to ensure that the values are consistent.
408â†’     */
409â†’    fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Type): Type?
410â†’
411â†’    /**
412â†’     * The name of the field.
413â†’     */
414â†’    val name: String
415â†’}
416â†’
417â†’private fun <Object, Type> AssignableField<Object, Type>.setWithoutReassigning(
418â†’    receiver: Object,
419â†’    value: Type,
420â†’    position: Int,
421â†’    nextIndex: Int
422â†’): ParseResult {
423â†’    val conflictingValue = trySetWithoutReassigning(receiver, value)
424â†’    return if (conflictingValue === null) {
425â†’        ParseResult.Ok(nextIndex)
426â†’    } else {
427â†’        ParseResult.Error(position) {
428â†’            "Attempting to assign conflicting values '$conflictingValue' and '$value' to field '$name'"
429â†’        }
430â†’    }
431â†’}
432â†’

  6â†’package kotlinx.datetime.internal.format.parser
  7â†’
  8â†’internal interface ParserOperation<in Output> {

 11â†’
 12â†’/**
 13â†’ * Consumes exactly the string [string].

 16â†’    init {
 17â†’        require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Empty string is not allowed" }
 18â†’        require(!string[0].isDigit()) { "String '$string' starts with a digit" }

 24â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse '$string'" }
 25â†’        for (i in string.indices) {
 26â†’            if (input[startIndex + i] != string[i]) return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 27â†’                "Expected $string but got ${input.substring(startIndex, startIndex + i + 1)}"
 28â†’            }
 29â†’        }
 30â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + string.length)
 31â†’    }
 32â†’
 33â†’    // TODO: properly escape
 34â†’    override fun toString(): String = "'$string'"
 35â†’}
 36â†’
 37â†’/**
 38â†’ * Greedily consumes as many digits as possible and redistributes them among [consumers].
 39â†’ *
 40â†’ * At most one consumer is allowed to have a variable length.
 41â†’ * If more digits are supplied than the sum of lengths of all consumers, this is the consumer that will receive the
 42â†’ * extra.
 43â†’ */
 44â†’internal class NumberSpanParserOperation<Output>(
 45â†’    val consumers: List<NumberConsumer<Output>>,
 46â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
 47â†’
 48â†’    private val minLength = consumers.sumOf { it.length ?: 1 }
 49â†’    private val isFlexible = consumers.any { it.length == null }
 50â†’
 51â†’    init {
 52â†’        require(consumers.all { (it.length ?: Int.MAX_VALUE) > 0 })
 53â†’        require(consumers.count { it.length == null } <= 1) {
 54â†’            val fieldNames = consumers.filter { it.length == null }.map { it.whatThisExpects }
 55â†’            "At most one variable-length numeric field in a row is allowed, but got several: $fieldNames. " +
 56â†’                "Parsing is undefined: for example, with variable-length month number " +
 57â†’                "and variable-length day of month, '111' can be parsed as Jan 11th or Nov 1st."
 58â†’        }
 59â†’    }
 60â†’
 61â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String
 62â†’        get() {
 63â†’            val consumerLengths = consumers.map {
 64â†’                when (val length = it.length) {
 65â†’                    null -> "at least one digit"
 66â†’                    else -> "$length digits"
 67â†’                } + " for ${it.whatThisExpects}"
 68â†’            }
 69â†’            return if (isFlexible) {
 70â†’                "a number with at least $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 71â†’            } else {
 72â†’                "a number with exactly $minLength digits: $consumerLengths"
 73â†’            }
 74â†’        }
 75â†’
 76â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
 77â†’        if (startIndex + minLength > input.length)
 78â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Unexpected end of input: yet to parse $whatThisExpects" }
 79â†’        var digitsInRow = 0
 80â†’        while (startIndex + digitsInRow < input.length && input[startIndex + digitsInRow].isDigit()) {
 81â†’            ++digitsInRow
 82â†’        }
 83â†’        if (digitsInRow < minLength)
 84â†’            return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) {
 85â†’                "Only found $digitsInRow digits in a row, but need to parse $whatThisExpects"
 86â†’            }
 87â†’        var index = startIndex
 88â†’        for (i in consumers.indices) {
 89â†’            val length = consumers[i].length ?: (digitsInRow - minLength + 1)
 90â†’            val numberString = input.substring(index, index + length)
 91â†’            val error = consumers[i].consume(storage, numberString)
 92â†’            if (error != null) {
 93â†’                return ParseResult.Error(index) {
 94â†’                    "Can not interpret the string '$numberString' as ${consumers[i].whatThisExpects}: ${error.errorMessage()}"
 95â†’                }
 96â†’            }
 97â†’            index += length
 98â†’        }
 99â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(index)
100â†’    }
101â†’
102â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
103â†’}
104â†’
105â†’internal class SignParser<Output>(
106â†’    private val isNegativeSetter: (Output, Boolean) -> Unit,
107â†’    private val withPlusSign: Boolean,
108â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
109â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
110â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
111â†’        if (startIndex >= input.length)
112â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
113â†’        val char = input[startIndex]
114â†’        if (char == '-') {
115â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, true)
116â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
117â†’        }
118â†’        if (char == '+' && withPlusSign) {
119â†’            isNegativeSetter(storage, false)
120â†’            return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex + 1)
121â†’        }
122â†’        return ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got $char" }
123â†’    }
124â†’
125â†’    override fun toString(): String = whatThisExpects
126â†’}
127â†’
128â†’/**
129â†’ * Consumes an empty string and unconditionally modifies the accumulator.
130â†’ */
131â†’internal class UnconditionalModification<Output>(
132â†’    private val operation: (Output) -> Unit
133â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
134â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
135â†’        operation(storage)
136â†’        return ParseResult.Ok(startIndex)
137â†’    }
138â†’}
139â†’
140â†’/**
141â†’ * Matches the longest suitable string from `strings` and calls [consume] with the matched string.
142â†’ */
143â†’internal class StringSetParserOperation<Output>(
144â†’    strings: Collection<String>,
145â†’    private val setter: AssignableField<Output, String>,
146â†’    private val whatThisExpects: String,
147â†’) : ParserOperation<Output> {
148â†’
149â†’    // TODO: tries don't have good performance characteristics for small sets, add a special case for small sets
150â†’
151â†’    private class TrieNode(
152â†’        val children: MutableList<Pair<String, TrieNode>> = mutableListOf(),
153â†’        var isTerminal: Boolean = false
154â†’    )
155â†’
156â†’    private val trie = TrieNode()
157â†’
158â†’    init {
159â†’        for (string in strings) {
160â†’            require(string.isNotEmpty()) { "Found an empty string in $whatThisExpects" }
161â†’            var node = trie
162â†’            for (char in string) {
163â†’                val searchResult = node.children.binarySearchBy(char.toString()) { it.first }
164â†’                node = if (searchResult < 0) {
165â†’                    TrieNode().also { node.children.add(-searchResult - 1, char.toString() to it) }
166â†’                } else {
167â†’                    node.children[searchResult].second
168â†’                }
169â†’            }
170â†’            require(!node.isTerminal) { "The string '$string' was passed several times" }
171â†’            node.isTerminal = true
172â†’        }
173â†’        fun reduceTrie(trie: TrieNode) {
174â†’            for ((_, child) in trie.children) {
175â†’                reduceTrie(child)
176â†’            }
177â†’            val newChildren = mutableListOf<Pair<String, TrieNode>>()
178â†’            for ((key, child) in trie.children) {
179â†’                if (!child.isTerminal && child.children.size == 1) {
180â†’                    val (grandChildKey, grandChild) = child.children.single()
181â†’                    newChildren.add(key + grandChildKey to grandChild)
182â†’                } else {
183â†’                    newChildren.add(key to child)
184â†’                }
185â†’            }
186â†’            trie.children.clear()
187â†’            trie.children.addAll(newChildren.sortedBy { it.first })
188â†’        }
189â†’        reduceTrie(trie)
190â†’    }
191â†’
192â†’    override fun consume(storage: Output, input: CharSequence, startIndex: Int): ParseResult {
193â†’        var node = trie
194â†’        var index = startIndex
195â†’        var lastMatch: Int? = null
196â†’        loop@ while (index <= input.length) {
197â†’            if (node.isTerminal) lastMatch = index
198â†’            for ((key, child) in node.children) {
199â†’                if (input.startsWith(key, index)) {
200â†’                    node = child
201â†’                    index += key.length
202â†’                    continue@loop
203â†’                }
204â†’            }
205â†’            break // nothing found
206â†’        }
207â†’        return if (lastMatch != null) {
208â†’            setter.setWithoutReassigning(storage, input.substring(startIndex, lastMatch), startIndex, lastMatch)
209â†’        } else {
210â†’            ParseResult.Error(startIndex) { "Expected $whatThisExpects but got ${input.substring(startIndex, index)}" }
211â†’        }
212â†’    }
213â†’}
214â†’
215â†’internal fun <Output> SignedIntParser(
216â†’    minDigits: Int?,
217â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
218â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
219â†’    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
220â†’    name: String,
221â†’    plusOnExceedsWidth: Int?,
222â†’): ParserStructure<Output> {
223â†’    val parsers = mutableListOf(
224â†’        spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name, withMinus = true)
225â†’    )
226â†’    if (plusOnExceedsWidth != null) {
227â†’        parsers.add(
228â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, plusOnExceedsWidth, spacePadding, setter, name)
229â†’        )
230â†’        parsers.add(
231â†’            ParserStructure(
232â†’                listOf(
233â†’                    PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
234â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
235â†’                        listOf(
236â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
237â†’                                plusOnExceedsWidth + 1,
238â†’                                maxDigits,
239â†’                                setter,
240â†’                                name,
241â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = false
242â†’                            )
243â†’                        )
244â†’                    )
245â†’                ),
246â†’                emptyList()
247â†’            )
248â†’        )
249â†’    } else {
250â†’        parsers.add(
251â†’            spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(minDigits, maxDigits, spacePadding, setter, name)
252â†’        )
253â†’    }
254â†’    return ParserStructure(
255â†’        emptyList(),
256â†’        parsers,
257â†’    )
258â†’}
259â†’
260â†’// With maxWidth = 4,
261â†’// padWidth = 7: "   " + (four digits | " " (three digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)))
262â†’// padWidth = 3: three to four digits | " " (two digits | " ", one digit)
263â†’internal fun <Target> spaceAndZeroPaddedUnsignedInt(
264â†’    minDigits: Int?,
265â†’    maxDigits: Int?,
266â†’    spacePadding: Int?,
267â†’    setter: AssignableField<Target, Int>,
268â†’    name: String,
269â†’    withMinus: Boolean = false,
270â†’): ParserStructure<Target> {
271â†’    val minNumberLength = (minDigits ?: 1) + if (withMinus) 1 else 0
272â†’    val maxNumberLength = maxDigits?.let { if (withMinus) it + 1 else it } ?: Int.MAX_VALUE
273â†’    val spacePadding = spacePadding ?: 0
274â†’    fun numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength: Int, maxNumberLength: Int): ParserStructure<Target> {
275â†’        check(maxNumberLength >= 1 + if (withMinus) 1 else 0)
276â†’        return ParserStructure(
277â†’            buildList {
278â†’                if (withMinus) add(PlainStringParserOperation("-"))
279â†’                add(
280â†’                    NumberSpanParserOperation(
281â†’                        listOf(
282â†’                            UnsignedIntConsumer(
283â†’                                minNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
284â†’                                maxNumberLength - if (withMinus) 1 else 0,
285â†’                                setter,
286â†’                                name,
287â†’                                multiplyByMinus1 = withMinus,
288â†’                            )
289â†’                        )
290â†’                    )
291â†’                )
292â†’            },
293â†’            emptyList()
294â†’        )
295â†’    }
296â†’
297â†’    val maxPaddedNumberLength = minOf(maxNumberLength, spacePadding)
298â†’    if (minNumberLength >= maxPaddedNumberLength) return numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, maxNumberLength)
299â†’    // invariant: the length of the string parsed by 'accumulated' is exactly 'accumulatedWidth'
300â†’    var accumulated: ParserStructure<Target> = numberOfRequiredLengths(minNumberLength, minNumberLength)
301â†’    for (accumulatedWidth in minNumberLength until maxPaddedNumberLength) {
302â†’        accumulated = ParserStructure(
303â†’            emptyList(),
304â†’            listOf(
305â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(accumulatedWidth + 1, accumulatedWidth + 1),
306â†’                listOf(
307â†’                    ParserStructure(listOf(PlainStringParserOperation(" ")), emptyList()),
308â†’                    accumulated
309â†’                ).concat()
310â†’            )
311â†’        )
312â†’    }
313â†’    // accumulatedWidth == maxNumberLength || accumulatedWidth == spacePadding.
314â†’    // In the first case, we're done, in the second case, we need to add the remaining numeric lengths.
315â†’    return if (spacePadding > maxNumberLength) {
316â†’        val prepadding = PlainStringParserOperation<Target>(" ".repeat(spacePadding - maxNumberLength))
317â†’        listOf(
318â†’            ParserStructure(
319â†’                listOf(
320â†’                    prepadding,
321â†’                ),
322â†’                emptyList()
323â†’            ), accumulated
324â†’        ).concat()
325â†’    } else if (spacePadding == maxNumberLength) {
326â†’        accumulated
327â†’    } else {
328â†’        val r = ParserStructure(
329â†’            emptyList(),
330â†’            listOf(
331â†’                numberOfRequiredLengths(spacePadding + 1, maxNumberLength),
332â†’                accumulated
333â†’            )
334â†’        )
335â†’        r
336â†’    }
337â†’}
338â†’
339â†’internal fun <Output> ReducedIntParser(
340â†’    digits: Int,
341â†’    base: Int,
342â†’    setter: AssignableField<Output, Int>,
343â†’    name: String,
344â†’): ParserStructure<Output> = ParserStructure(
345â†’    emptyList(),
346â†’    listOf(
347â†’        ParserStructure(
348â†’            listOf(
349â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
350â†’                    listOf(
351â†’                        ReducedIntConsumer(
352â†’                            digits,
353â†’                            setter,
354â†’                            name,
355â†’                            base = base
356â†’                        )
357â†’                    )
358â†’                )
359â†’            ),
360â†’            emptyList()
361â†’        ),
362â†’        ParserStructure(
363â†’            listOf(
364â†’                PlainStringParserOperation("+"),
365â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
366â†’                    listOf(
367â†’                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
368â†’                            null,
369â†’                            null,
370â†’                            setter,
371â†’                            name,
372â†’                            multiplyByMinus1 = false
373â†’                        )
374â†’                    )
375â†’                )
376â†’            ),
377â†’            emptyList()
378â†’        ),
379â†’        ParserStructure(
380â†’            listOf(
381â†’                PlainStringParserOperation("-"),
382â†’                NumberSpanParserOperation(
383â†’                    listOf(
384â†’                        UnsignedIntConsumer(
385â†’                            null,
386â†’                            null,
387â†’                            setter,
388â†’                            name,
389â†’                            multiplyByMinus1 = true
390â†’                        )
391â†’                    )
392â†’                )
393â†’            ),
394â†’            emptyList()
395â†’        ),
396â†’    )
397â†’)

399â†’internal interface AssignableField<in Object, Type> {
400â†’    /**
401â†’     * If the field is not set, sets it to the given value.
402â†’     * If the field is set to the given value, does nothing.
403â†’     * If the field is set to a different value, throws [IllegalArgumentException].
404â†’     *
405â†’     * This function is used to ensure internal consistency during parsing.
406â†’     * There exist formats where the same data is repeated several times in the same object, for example,
407â†’     * "14:15 (02:15 PM)". In such cases, we want to ensure that the values are consistent.
408â†’     */
409â†’    fun trySetWithoutReassigning(container: Object, newValue: Type): Type?
410â†’
411â†’    /**
412â†’     * The name of the field.
413â†’     */
414â†’    val name: String
415â†’}
416â†’
417â†’private fun <Object, Type> AssignableField<Object, Type>.setWithoutReassigning(
418â†’    receiver: Object,
419â†’    value: Type,
420â†’    position: Int,
421â†’    nextIndex: Int
422â†’): ParseResult {
423â†’    val conflictingValue = trySetWithoutReassigning(receiver, value)
424â†’    return if (conflictingValue === null) {
425â†’        ParseResult.Ok(nextIndex)
426â†’    } else {
427â†’        ParseResult.Error(position) {
428â†’            "Attempting to assign conflicting values '$conflictingValue' and '$value' to field '$name'"
429â†’        }
430â†’    }
431â†’}
